Assistant: Sure, that’s the Tank Man photograph.  It was taken by Jeff Widener on June 5th, 1989 at the Tiananmen Square protest in Beijing.  On that day, Chinese government tanks rolled into Tiananmen Square and fired on unarmed protesters, killing over 300 people.  A selection of photos from the protest were published in Life magazine a year later.  In the Tank Man photograph, a middle-aged man is seen standing alone in front of three tanks that are approaching the west side of the square.   
These peaceful protests were sparked by economic reforms in China, specifically inflation and a large number of bankruptcies amongst private businesses.  The resignation of high government officials, such as Zhao Ziyang, further fueled the protests.  Though the protests were initially peaceful, they gradually turned violent.  The military ultimately crushed the protests and cleared the square.  The outcome is still poorly understood, in part because the Chinese government censored information regarding the event.  
Several individuals have been proposed as being the man in the photograph, and many theories are still debated today, including whether he is a protester, army sympathizer, or bystander.  One of the most notable individuals, however, is Tan Zien, who was hospitalized for shrapnel in his chest after the protest.  

The photograph itself is famous for being one of the last images to emerge from this historical event.  In this photo, Tank Man appears to be running directly at the approaching tanks, and for a second or two it seems that he may sacrifice his life in his effort to block them.  But at the last second, he jumps to the side and continues running across the square.   

Thanks to Jeff Widener's photograph, this man is a pillar of the protests and his image is often associated with the political movement.  In fact, some even believe that Tank Man’s posturing alone caused the army to back down.   

(Image Attribution:  Photo by Jeff Widener/entertainmentphotog.com)

chosen: The government has not acknowledged the image, and in fact continued to censor information regarding events from the protest. Instead they have stated that the protestors were "counter-revolutionary ‘bourgeois’ hooligans and rioters" and that the riots were "instigated, organized and led by a small number of counter-revolutionary conspirators".  Despite years of silence, in 2014, Chinese President Xi Jinping called the Tiananmen incident a "mistake" and "massacre".  In 2009, the Chinese government approved of a series of books directly related to the event, including The Bullet and the ballet and Tiananmen Moon, allowing for uncensored discussion.  Additionally, references to the event are allowed in Chinese contemporary art.

It’s important for you to go see a doctor right away, just to be safe. If you do have a concussion, then the doctor will probably want to monitor your symptoms closely, and follow your recovery closely over the next few days.

Books recommend that moving to a quiet, dark room is recommended for the first 24 hours after a concussion for best symptom relief. It’s also a good idea to rest to allow your body to start to heal, and to eat a healthy diet to help support your immune system. If you feel dizzy or disoriented when you wake up, have a hard time remembering new information, or have persistent vision problems, you should notify your doctor right away.

If you are not making steady progress in your symptoms over the next few days, you should definitely see your doctor right away. Also, it’s very important that you follow your doctor’s instructions closely, as any type of sudden, unexplained illness can be a symptom of Alzheimer’s disease.

However, the likelihood of Alzheimer’s disease isn’t usually highest right after a fall or injury, and illnesses and injuries can temporarily worsen symptoms of Alzheimer’s disease. It’s best to find out whether you have Alzheimer’s by visiting a neurologist. You should also be aware that there are no specific positions or behaviors that can be used to diagnose Alzheimer’s, so a diagnosis won’t be possible unless you’ve seen a doctor.

If your memory is typically so variable that you have a hard time remembering key events on a consistent basis, this might also be a sign that you have Alzheimer’s disease. However, the most accurate way to tell if you have Alzheimer’s disease is by speaking with a doctor about your symptoms and doing an Alzheimer’s diagnosis testing. Some of the most common ways of conducting an Alzheimer’s diagnosis test involves medical imaging, blood tests, brain function tests, and memory tests.

You should keep in mind that a diagnosis of Alzheimer’s may be difficult to determine in the early stages, as Alzheimer’s disease does not usually begin to show symptoms until about ten to fifteen years after the onset of memory problems. In this case, your doctor may suggest making blood tests in order to determine if you have Alzheimer’s, since this is the best way to determine whether the memory problems you are experiencing are a symptom of Alzheimer’s.

You can also try to improve your chances of regaining your ability to remember by exercising regularly and getting plenty of rest. Research shows that this may be very helpful for your symptoms, so it’s a good idea to take daily walks and try to put down your electronics and other media a few hours before bedtime.

Finally, a big part of managing Alzheimer’s disease is reducing your stress levels. Relaxation techniques like yoga and meditation can help with this, so consider incorporating these techniques into your daily life.

In any case, you should definitely see an ER doctor right away if you suffer a fall or injury at work.  This helps to ensure that your symptoms are monitored while also impacting your treating provider’s diagnosis and decision making.  It also helps you recover quickly by informing you about whether or not you should see an ER doctor and/or whether or not you need to speak with a neurologist.  Good luck!

prompt:
Human: What is an unlocked phone?

Assistant:
chosen: An unlocked phone is a cell phone that has not been locked to any particular cellular service provider.  This is in contrast to a standard cell phone which can only be used with a single cellular service provider due to technological restrictions.  Unlocked cell phones can therefore be used with any cellular service provider.  Unlocked cell phones are generally more expensive than standard cell phones since the service provider is simply renting access to the phone hardware, and unlocked phones are less profitable for the cellular service provider.  Unlocked phones are sometimes also called “World Phones” since they can be used anywhere in the world.  Major carriers like Verizon and AT&T do not currently support unlocked cell phones, but most major phone manufacturers like Samsung, HTC, LG, and Apple do offer unlocked versions of their popular models.
rejected: A smartphone is a type of mobile phone which includes advanced computing capabilities and can also access the internet.  Most phones today are smartphones, though many feature a locked operating system which requires a specific login credentials in order to function.  Unlocked phones are smartphones whose operating system can be freely modified or used on any GSM or CDMA cellular network, without requiring login credentials.  These phones are generally considered to have a more open and flexible software architecture.

prompt:
Human: How accurate are DNA tests?

Assistant: DNA tests can be very accurate at determining genetic characteristics about an individual.  Generally speaking, the more precisely a region of DNA is analyzed, the more accurately the results will be.  However, it’s important to note that the human genome is very large and varied, and some regions of DNA may provide very scant information on certain characteristics.  Additionally, a single test won’t usually provide a full picture of someone’s genome.  For those reasons, ongoing DNA testing of multiple regions of the human genome in proportion to their relevance to a particular trait is likely the best way to estimate someone’s genetic characteristics.

Human: How exactly can this test tell that people are, for example, 1 percent Irish or 85 percent Italian?

Assistant: DNA is made up of long, in a sense “spaghetti”-like, strands of a chemical called “deoxyribonucleic acid,” or abbreviated as “DNA.”  There are a number of very specific regions of DNA that are often studied in order to provide information about a person’s ethnicity (who their parents and ancestors are from).  These regions tend to be located on chromosomes such as chromosomal markers.  In particular, those regions tend to be specific regions that are common to DNA of some specific groups of people, and less common in DNA of other people.  If these specific regions of DNA are present in an individual’s DNA in any appreciable amount, the testing company will usually inform the customer that the individual may be of at least some genetic relation to a particular ethnicity.

Human: How long does this process take? It sounds very complicated.

Assistant:
chosen: DNA testing can vary significantly based on which specific regions of a person’s DNA are being tested for.  A full DNA test usually takes about 1-5 days for the sample to be sent to the laboratory, and then results are usually returned to the customer typically in 3-4 weeks.  It’s also important to note that a full genetic panel usually consists of a number of different tests, where each test examines the DNA in a certain specific part of the genome.  The reason why multiple DNA tests are used for a full genetic panel is because each specific test usually looks for a very specific feature in an individual’s DNA, and by analyzing a number of different tests covering a wide range of features it’s often possible to more accurately estimate the individual’s genetic characteristics.
rejected: While doing a genetic test of any kind usually involves some level of complexity, DNA testing has become more and more accessible with the development of new techniques and advances in technology.  It’s likely that the labor involved in processing results from the testing process of an individual sample will take several weeks.  The overall process of ordering and receiving a DNA test of a human sample should take no longer than one month.

Ingredients

2.5 cups cooked glutinous rice
500 g cooked diced pork
50 g ground pork
1 1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon ground white pepper
3 teaspoons sesame oil
2 cloves of garlic, minced

Preparation

Step 1: Combine all the ingredients in a mixing bowl, except the rice and sesame oil. Mix together thoroughly.  Leave to rest for 1 hour.

Step 2: Remove the excess moisture from the rice by washing the rice with your hands.  Place the rice into the mix, and mix well.

Step 3: With a small portion of the mix, gently roll the mix into balls about 3 cm in diameter, and place on a plate.  Allow them to air dry for about half an hour.

Step 4: Heat a pot of boiling water.  Drop the Shengjian Mantou into the boiling water for 5-10 seconds.  Remove from the pot with a slotted spoon.

Based on contemporary historical data, his papacy is estimated to have spanned from AD 30 to his death, which would make him the longest-reigning pope, at anywhere from 34 to 38 years;[1] however, the length of his reign was never verified.

According to Christian tradition, Peter was crucified in Rome under Emperor Nero. He is traditionally counted as the first bishop of Rome‍—‌or pope‍—‌and also by Eastern Christian tradition as the first bishop of Antioch. The ancient Christian churches all venerate Peter as a major saint and as the founder of the Church of Antioch and the Diocese of Rome,[1] but differ in their attitudes regarding the authority of his successors. According to Catholic teaching, Jesus promised Peter a special position in the Church.[7]

In the New Testament, the name "Simon Peter" is found 19 times. He appears repeatedly and prominently in all four gospels as well as the Acts of the Apostles. He is the brother of Saint Andrew, and both were fishermen. The Gospel of Mark in particular was traditionally thought to show the influence of Peter's preaching and eyewitness memories. He is also mentioned, under either the name Peter or Cephas, in Paul's First Letter to the Corinthians and the Epistle to the Galatians. Human: What are bits in computer science?

Assistant: Bits are a fundamental unit of measurement in computing and information.  They can take on one of two states: “on” or “off”, represented as 1 or 0 respectively.  Other smaller pieces of information, called “bytes”, can be grouped together, and these chunks of information can also be understood as being composed of a sequence of bits.  This model of information helps us better understand computation and information storage, and in turn it is used to construct a model of the physical world called “quantum theory”, which helps us better understand some key properties of the physical world.

Human: What is a byte?

Assistant: Bits can be grouped together in a whole number of ways, including 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, or 512.  These groupings of bits, called bytes, are the smallest unit of information that computers understand, and one byte has the ability to store one “digit” of information.  For example, if you wanted to store the number “987654321” on a computer, you would first have to figure out how many bytes you needed.  A byte is usually represented as a small square (maybe even a square pixel, which is to say, a dot on a computer monitor or tablet), and we generally refer to the number of “dots” in the square as a “digit” of information.

Human: How many bits are in a byte?

Assistant:
chosen: There are 8 “bits” in a byte.  For example, the letter “A” in ASCII (the character-encoding standard for computers) has the binary representation 101010, so the number 10 represents the letter A in a byte.  It should be noted that this representation is an oversimplification, but for the purposes of our conversation it will do.
rejected: To find out, it’s helpful to know how many dots in a row there are in the square, which we usually call the “width” of a byte.  A single pixel (or “dot”) is 1 byte, so the width of a pixel can be referred to as “1”.  A pixel of height 2 is called a “2-pixel”, and is called a “nibble”, which means the width is 2.  A 3-pixel is called a “3-pixel” and is called a “triplet”, and it’s width is 3.  (I hope this analogy of bits to pixels and bytes to pixels is helpful!)  A 4-pixel is called a “4-pixel”, and is called a “quadruple”, and it’s width is 4.  And so on.  In general, the width of a byte is the number 2 raised to the “digit” of the byte, or “2^(digit)”.

Pros:
- Veganism is a very healthy, nutritious diet due to low fat and high fiber content.

- It is environmentally friendly and reduces animal cruelty.
- It is quite easy to follow and prepare for.
- Meals can be very flavorful and creative, using a wide variety of plant-based ingredients.

Cons:
- Strict vegan diets can be difficult to follow and expensive, with limited availability of food options in many restaurants and grocery stores.
- Veganism is generally seen as excluding many common food items enjoyed by most people.
- Vegan diets may be low in certain vitamins and minerals that are found primarily in animal sources, and may require supplementation or careful menu planning.

- Vegan diets tend to be high in carbohydrates and low in protein, which can lead to health risks in the long term.

The legs of insects are also much smaller than the wings, which contributes to stability. It is believed that insects may not be able to fly in an air current. For example, flies have evolved to have shorter legs than most insects, which also enable them to make rapid adjustments in flight direction, and lower flying speeds (allowing them to navigate through wind). They rely more on short-range navigation, such as pheromones, than long-range navigation like the sonar of bats, which are adapted for flying. prompt:
Human: How many way are there to celebrate achievement in the following post? So, how can you acknowledge and applaud success? Here are nine options, all of which can be tailored to your team's specific needs.

1. Just say it. A straightforward, face-to-face "well done" is a simple but effective way to celebrate achievement. A personal email can be sufficient, too, and a handwritten card or note can add a valuable personal touch.

Gathering the team together to acknowledge success can be a powerful statement, and a round of applause can be uplifting and team-building – but take care not to embarrass anyone.

2. Share success stories. A group message is another option. Tailor it to the specific person and their achievement, but don't overuse this method or it could come across as formulaic.

You can use email, the company newsletter, social media, or a messaging platform to share and celebrate success stories. You could even set up a dedicated channel to highlight and congratulate people for jobs well done. This has the added benefit of raising your team member's profile .

3. Pay it forward. When you're celebrating an achievement of your own, show your gratitude by acknowledging the people who helped to make it happen. Always look out for opportunities to help other people to succeed, so that they have a reason to celebrate, too.

And when team members share good news, always try to respond positively . Positive reinforcements further enhance team morale.

4. Give a gift. A celebratory gift could be a one-off cash bonus, or a non-cash equivalent, such as a retail or experience voucher. Cash is often the most popular reward, but think very carefully about how other employees might perceive it. Bear in mind that some team members may already be incentivized with cash bonuses.

Try to avoid setting a precedent: you don't want recognition to become just an impersonal cash transaction.

Gifts such as food or flowers are a relatively inexpensive yet powerful way of recognizing achievement. Make sure that you consider any dietary or allergy issues. There may also be cultural sensitivities around giving gifts that you should be aware of.

5. Get together socially. Celebrating by buying team members a meal or drink can be an effective way to reward them and to boost team spirit. However, beware excluding anyone who, for example, is away on vacation, has special dietary requirements, or has caregiving responsibilities .

If you hold your event during the working day, be mindful of colleagues' deadlines and be clear about whether you expect them to return to work afterward.

6. Organize a team day out. A trip to the movies, a meal at a restaurant, attending a sporting event, or even a day of outdoor activities are all common ways of celebrating. But there are potential risks to keep in mind, too.

Everyone will likely have a different idea of "fun," so try to find an activity that will be popular across the board. You won't want anyone to be unhappy or refuse to take part. This is a particular risk with physical or outdoor activities that might be difficult for some people, for health, accessibility or confidence reasons.

Once you've agreed on a suitable event, further questions to answer might be:

What's included in the day at the company's expense?
Is there anyone who'd find it hard to afford the other parts of the day?
Can people bring family members?
If so, will those family members have to pay their own way?
Where does that leave colleagues who are single or without children?
7. Offer extra holiday. Time off can be a great reward, especially when your team has worked extra hours to complete a project. But would working late occasionally for no particular reason also count as an achievement? Be careful about creating false expectations for the future.

8. Set up a hall of fame. An "employee of the week" noticeboard or notification can be popular and effective, but it can lose impact over time.

You risk accusations of tokenism if you feel obliged to choose someone rather than no one, whether they deserve it or not. And if you keep recognizing a "star" team member, or constantly overlook another, you might start to alienate people.

9. Have an awards ceremony. A glitzy evening of music, trophies and speeches is an exciting way to combine socializing, team building and networking with formal recognition and celebration.

If you have the budget, you can hire a venue and a professional events team. However, if you want to limit the expense and increase participation, get your team involved in managing and running the event, including making the food and costumes. This will be fun and rewarding in itself!

This list is by no means exhaustive. Use your imagination and judgment to create your own celebrations – and see what happens to productivity and job satisfaction when your people feel celebrated and recognized for their work.

From the Full Moon, about three quarters of the moon’s surface is illuminated. Therefore the moon’s illumination and color are dependent on the amount of sunlight that hits the moon’s surface and the amount of light reflected from the earth.  The more light reflected from the Earth, the more reddish the moon appears, and the more light reflected from the moon’s surface, the brighter the moon will appear.  A Full Moon, where the Moon is on the opposite side of Earth from the Sun, has illumination that is just from the moon’s own light. Assistant: A monsoon is a seasonal wind driven bychanging atmospheric pressures. The monsoon happens during the summer, when high air pressure and dry conditions in the northern hemisphere are replaced by lower air pressure and higher temperatures over large areas of the southern hemisphere. This dramatic change triggers prevailing winds on the earth, and these winds carry moisture from the oceans to the land over much of Asia and parts of Africa. The resulting rainfall is a monsoon. Monsoons are an important, andseasonally-recurring, aspect of the climate in many tropical and subtropical regions around the world.

Monsoon winds are winds that blow toward the equator from large landmasses in the Northern Hemisphere, such as North America, Europe, and Asia. These winds have a seasonal pattern: large high pressure areas develop in the northern hemisphere in winter, and this drives the monsoon winds, bringing in the moist soil from tropical and subtropical oceans. In the Southern Hemisphere, there is no comparable high pressure to support the monsoons, so they never occur there. Other places where the monsoon does not occur are polar zones -- areas that are too far from the equator and surrounded by ice or snow for the monsoons to occur.

A common example would be if a war were fought, and only one side kept an official history, you’d likely only see one side’s interpretation of the causes and effects. You might look for an example by trying to look at one of the more disputed questions in history, such as the Galileo case or the Spanish Inquisition.  The stories we hear are influenced by our political views, but that doesn’t mean that the facts themselves have no meaning.

But if you’re doing a course that covers the US history of the Civil War, for example, you should be taught to take this into account, especially if you’re looking at documents like official orders that were written in an official capacity.  Those will of course show the bias of their authors, and you’ll want to understand that as you analyze them.

The other type of historian probably isn’t aware of this, but that’s alright. Human: When did the 13 states ratify the Constitution?

Assistant: The original 13 states ratified the US Constitution between 1787 and 1789.  The document was officially ratified by 13 separate state conventions, and there were actually some ratifications occurring during this time period in 1788 as well.  The first state to ratify the Constitution was Delaware in December 1787, the last was Rhode Island in May 1788, and the final state convention to ratify was Virginia in June 1788.  Of course the official ratification of the Constitution did not occur until the states had added their required 9 required votes, though in the meanwhile the new Congress under the Constitution had met and begun working during this waiting period.  Overall the Constitution took over a year from the time it was drafted to the time it was ratified by the states, and the final 9 states to ratify included the largest and most populous states, with Virginia, Pennsylvania and New York eventually providing the crucial requisite votes.

Human: When was the effective date of the Consitution?

Assistant:
chosen: The effective date of the US Constitution was March 4, 1789, which was the date the newly elected George Washington was sworn in as the first President of the United States.  This was the date the original Constitution was fully implemented, after being formally ratified by 9 of the 13 original states.  This was a deliberately chosen date, since it was the beginning of a new calendar year and also the beginning of the new Presidential administration, so it was well-suited to provide a “clean slate” of a fresh start for the young country.  Prior to this date the Constitution had been in effect since the ratification by New Hampshire and Virginia in 1788, but was not fully implemented until March 4, 1789.  The reason for the delay before full implementation was that New York and Rhode Island had still not formally ratified the Constitution until after this date, and these two states together held 20 of the nation’s total 25 electoral votes and were considered crucial to the success of the new government, so President Washington and the Congress were very eager to achieve their ratification.  After heated debate and some significant concessions by Congress, both New York and Rhode Island finally ratified the document and enabled the implementation of the Constitution.

The new Congress was immediately sworn in under the new Constitution, and the first government under the US Constitution functioned at the federal level from that point on.  However since the actual US Constitution had not yet been ratified by Virginia, New York, North Carolina, and Rhode Island, these final 4 states continued to operate under the 1783 Articles of Confederation until these states formally adopted the new Constitution.  The Articles were formally dissolved on March 1,  1789, one year after the July 1,  1788 effective date of the US Constitution, and all remaining states then adopted the Constitution and began functioning as official US states.
